item.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
item.description: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

item.title: Great white shark captured on video in Mediterranean in rare sighting
item.description: Great white sharks are classified as "critically endangered" in the Mediterranean Sea, and underwater sightings are incredibly rare.

item.title: U.S. launches largest-ever effort to denaturalize citizens accused of fraud
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday announced it is seeking to revoke the citizenship of 17 U.S. citizens accused of immigration fraud.

item.title: Biggest winners from the 79th Tony Awards
item.description: CBS News New York's Dave Carlin breaks down the biggest wins and performances from the 79th Tony Awards.

item.title: Trump storms off during NBC interview after insisting California primaries were rigged
item.description: President Trump continues to make claims about the California primaries and the subsequent vote count. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more on his latest reaction to questions about his election fraud claims.

item.title: Raman outpaces Pratt in LA mayor contest; California governor's race unsettled
item.description: Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman surged past Spencer Pratt on Sunday in the race for LA mayor. The California governor's race remains undecided.

item.title: U.K. teen banned from Russia after exposing alleged crypto scheme
item.description: Russia sanctioned Alexander Browder, son of financier-turned-activist Bill Browder, after he unveiled an alleged cryptocurrency money laundering network.

item.title: What happens when Social Security recipients are sued over unpaid debt?
item.description: If a creditor sues you in retirement, understanding the process could help you avoid some costly mistakes.

item.title: At least 5 people stabbed at Penn Station, police say
item.description: Six people were taken to the hospital after a stabbing incident at New York City's Penn Station Sunday evening.

item.title: Trump administration targets citizens accused of fraud, other crimes in denaturalization effort
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to revoke the citizenship of 17 people accused of fraud and other crimes in a denaturalization effort. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: Border czar Tom Homan says immigration arrests are not a priority for ICE at World Cup
item.description: White House border czar Tom Homan told CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez that ICE agents at the World Cup will not be focused on immigration arrests.

item.title: Lawmakers fear Bill Pulte's political motives amid appointment as acting intelligence chief
item.description: Connecticut Democratic Rep. Jim Himes told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that President Trump's appointment of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence was his "most dangerous." CBS News' Olivia Gazis has more.

item.title: Do Maine voters care about Graham Platner's scandals and controversial past?
item.description: Graham Platner remains defiant as he prepares for the Maine Democratic primary election. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more on how voters are reacting to the scandals plaguing his campaign.

item.title: Massive earthquake rocks the Philippines and causes small tsunami
item.description: The largest earthquake to hit the Philippines in more than three decades killed dozens and triggered a small tsunami. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: Can a wage garnishment be taken from severance pay?
item.description: Job transitions often come with severance pay, but they can also come with unexpected financial complications.

item.title: Latest news on Iran war after Israel and Tehran exchange strikes
item.description: Tensions rose in the Middle East following strikes between Iran and Israel. President Trump is urging both sides to stop "shooting," amid ongoing tensions involving Hezbollah and the Houthis in the region. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Olivia Rinaldi report.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: National security expert on possible threats as Trump prepares for Knicks-Spurs NBA Finals Game 3
item.description: President Trump is planning to attend Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs. The NYPD has ramped up security for the game. Charles Marino, a former Secret Service special agent and adviser for the Department of Homeland Security, joins with more.

item.title: Flooding could impact more than 88 million as storms threaten multiple states
item.description: States across the Midwest and northern Plains could see flooding as severe storms hit, forecasters say.

item.title: Republican, Democratic strategists on Graham Platner scandals, elections ahead of midterms
item.description: Political strategists Kevin Sheridan and Hyma Moore join CBS News with their take ahead of Graham Platner's primary in Maine, and President Trump's reaction to the California primaries during an interview with NBC.

item.title: Man honors former coach who helped him "learn how to believe" in himself
item.description: CBS News contributor David Begnaud went to New Orleans to ask everyday Americans who believed in them. He spoke with 23-year-old Chris Wurl who described how a former coach made a major impact on his life.

item.title: Graham Platner heads into Maine primary despite high-profile scandals
item.description: Democratic candidate Graham Platner is barreling into Maine's Senate primary despite multiple scandals plaguing his campaign. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns spoke to voters about his candidacy.

item.title: Steven Spielberg talks "Disclosure Day" and says aliens "have been here and they are here"
item.description: Oscar-winning director Steven Spielberg talks to CBS News about his 34th feature film, "Disclosure Day," which explores what would happen if all of the evidence about UFOs and alien life was released to the entire world and proved we are not alone. He also discusses his opinion on aliens, saying they "have been here and they are here."

item.title: Philippines earthquake kills at least 32 people, unleashes small tsunami
item.description: A deadly earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing dozens of people and sending small tsunami waves toward at least three nations.

item.title: Pope Leo denounces what he calls "scourge" of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy
item.description: Pope Leo XIV denounced the "scourge" of sexual violence by Catholic clergy and called for a "culture of care" in the Church ahead of an expected private meeting with victims in Spain.

item.title: Trump says U.S. shipbuilding is in a crisis as it lags far behind China
item.description: China rolls out over 1,000 cargo ships a year, while the U.S. – maybe three. The Trump administration has called this a crisis with both economic and national security risks.

item.title: Dog aging research may help canines, people live longer and healthier
item.description: The Dog Aging Project is working to help dogs live longer, healthier lives. The research results may help humans age well, too.

item.title: Teen dies after showing symptoms of heat-related illness on Grand Canyon hike
item.description: An 18-year-old died last week on a hike deep in the Grand Canyon, after showing symptoms of heat-related illness, the National Park Service said.

item.title: Alexander Zverev becomes Grand Slam champion with French Open win
item.description: In his fourth major final, Alexander Zverev beat Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7 (5), 6-1 for the French Open title on Sunday.

item.title: Russian teen claims first Grand Slam tennis title with French Open win
item.description: The eighth-ranked Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the French Open final on Saturday.
